sections.paragraph:
ἀπροσδόκητος, ον (προσδεκτέος ‘one must admit/expect’; Aeschyl., Hdt. et al.; SIG 742, 9; 814, 9; PFay 19, 3; Wsd 17:14; 3 Macc 3:8 al.; Philo, De Prov. in Eus., PE 8, 14, 60; Jos., Ant. 12, 308) unexpected ἀ. εὐεργεσίαι Dg 9:5.—DELG s.v. δέχομαι.

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

PFay PFay = Fayûm Towns and Their Papyri—List 4

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 125
